text: The surgeon is sewing the fascial defect in the midline, re-approximating the linea alba in an ETEP case. The needle driver in the right hand sutures at an awkward angle, while the fenestrated bipolar in the left hand approaches at a favorable angle, aiding the suturing process.

The surgeon inverts the image and reassigns the instruments, operating the fenestrated bipolar with the right hand and the needle driver with the left hand. This adjustment allows the fenestrated bipolar to approach the tissue at a better angle, facilitating easier suturing.

The surgeon runs the suture back multiple times to sew the posterior rectus sheath edges together, using the current instrument setup for easier suturing. The suture is trimmed, and the image is returned to its right-side-up orientation.
